concept: Eighty-One-Group / Fourth and Highest Ternary Scale
description: A noun for a group of eighty-one (3⁴), the fourth and highest scale in Phi's ternary numeral system. 'Ta rei' is exactly 81, while bare 'rei' before a noun gives an approximate magnitude around eighty-one and often has the pragmatic force of 'a great many' or 'too many to count for this purpose.' It never means literal infinity or uncountability. Exact composition continues through 242.
sound_symbolism: 'Re' opens with the light liquid 'r', and the hiatus 'e.i' rises from mid to high across two distinct syllables—a small ascent fitting a word that stands at the top of the counting scale.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a scale noun representing eighty-one. 'Ta rei' is exactly 81 and 'wi rei' is exactly 162; lower scales and units may follow. Bare 'rei' before a noun is approximate, as in 'rei silero' (stars on roughly an eighty-one-group scale; a great many stars). It appears at most once and is never recursively counted. With all lower coefficients at 'wi', the exact system ends at 'wi rei wi lau wi phoi wi shao wi' (242). Never combine a numeral or quantified phrase with plural 'lo'.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Bare 'rei' can support reflection on situations where exact counting is unnecessary or unavailable. It does not claim that phenomena, causes, or beings are inherently uncountable.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Repeated natural patterns can exceed what a viewer chooses to tally; bare 'rei' offers an approximate scale for that experience without calling it infinite.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Restricted and grouped numeral practices exist in human languages, but no single threshold represents traditional societies. In Phi, 'rei' is a designed highest scale with both exact and approximate uses.

tags.aesthetic: Can present a large visible pattern through approximate magnitude rather than an unsupported exact tally
tags.cognition: Names a learned group of eighty-one, not a perceptual or cognitive limit
tags.nature: Can estimate large natural quantities without claiming infinity
tags.wisdom: Keeps exact eighty-one, approximate eighty-one-scale, and literal uncountability distinct
